intent.operator_problem: Financial authoring stores have no guaranteed delivery path to downstream projection consumers, leaving visit-level financial aggregate completeness at unknown and forcing consumers toward direct authoring-store polling or stale data.
intent.pilot_fit: Wave 1 established authority and completeness envelopes, but lifecycle-aware complete/partial signals require durable event propagation first. This is the minimal infrastructure slice that unblocks Wave 2 projection consumers.
success_outcomes: OUT-1: Class A authoring writes and finance_outbox rows commit in the same database transaction with no partial state window.
OUT-2: Class B authoring writes and finance_outbox rows commit with parity to Class A under ADR-055.
OUT-3: Committed finance_outbox rows survive relay worker crash or restart and are delivered on a later poll cycle.
OUT-4: Duplicate delivery of the same event_id produces exactly one consumer side effect.
OUT-5: Projection consumers can rebuild from finance_outbox history to an equivalent live projection state under the same ordered event history.
OUT-6: Every finance_outbox row carries fact_class, origin_label, table_id, aggregate_id, and event_id without payload inference.
OUT-7: origin_label remains immutable from authoring boundary to consumer.
OUT-8: event_type values are centrally governed through the Wave 2 event catalog before producer or consumer use.
explicit_exclusions: No external consumer contract, public event bus, third-party event semantics, or reconciliation interface.
No event sourcing; outbox history is propagation infrastructure, not authoritative state reconstruction.
No authoritative totals, financial settlement, Total Drop, shift-end settlement, or final money position.
No CDC, WAL replication, Debezium, pg_logical, or post-pilot stream infrastructure.
No UI changes, components, panels, or routes.
No compliance domain scope; mtl_entry and MTLService remain parallel and isolated.
No reconciliation logic on fills and credits.
No player_id on Class B rows; table_buyin_telemetry and matching outbox rows use player_id = null.
No shared-parent table unification of Class A and Class B authoring stores.
No new projection surfaces; shift telemetry, session summaries, and pit dashboards are downstream slices.
No trigger-based outbox insertion for the pilot.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Triggers fire inside the database transaction boundary and could satisfy atomicity with less RPC change.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Rejected for pilot because hidden propagation behavior reduces rollback/debug visibility, creates trigger-creep risk, and conflicts with RPC-centric architecture.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: This is the direct operator value unlocked by the outbox.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Projection consumers are consequences of the transport layer and belong to the next slice.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Change streams reduce polling lag and read the database log directly.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Adds non-trivial post-pilot infrastructure and violates YAGNI for the Wave 2 pilot.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: An internal event relay can look like a natural integration point for external stakeholders.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Externalization requires separate ADR and stakeholder discovery; this FIB is internal-only.

adjacent_rejected.why_considered: Push delivery appears to remove poll interval lag.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Requires persistent PostgreSQL connection behavior incompatible with the current serverless model and still needs polling for recovery.

adjacent_rejected.idea: Shared-parent table for Class A and Class B authoring stores
adjacent_rejected.why_considered: A unified store could simplify the relay worker poll target.
adjacent_rejected.why_rejected_now: Q2 resolved that behavioral convergence risk outweighs pilot-scale structural benefit.

entities.name: finance_outbox
entities.description: Internal Projection Input event log written atomically with Class A, Class B, and Dependency Event authoring RPCs.

entities.name: processed_messages
entities.description: Consumer idempotency record for single internal consumer path assumed by Wave 2.

entities.name: table_buyin_telemetry
entities.description: Class B operational authoring store for table-level buy-in/grind observations; player_id absent by construction.

entities.name: FinancialOutboxEventDTO
entities.description: Canonical event envelope consumed by relay worker and downstream projection consumers.

entities.name: Wave 2 event catalog
entities.description: Central registry that must explicitly declare every allowed event_type before producer RPCs or consumers use it.

capabilities.name: atomic-producer-insertion
capabilities.description: Insert the authoring row and matching finance_outbox row within one database transaction for every in-scope producer path.

capabilities.name: polling-relay-delivery
capabilities.description: Poll undelivered finance_outbox rows, claim rows safely under concurrency, and deliver events with at-least-once semantics.

capabilities.name: consumer-idempotency
capabilities.description: Use processed_messages to ensure duplicate delivery of an event_id produces no duplicate side effect.

capabilities.name: ordered-replay
capabilities.description: Allow projection consumers to replay finance_outbox history in ordered event sequence and rebuild equivalent projection state.

capabilities.name: event-type-governance
capabilities.description: Register every event_type in the Wave 2 event catalog before using it in producer RPCs or consumers.

bounded_contexts: Projection consumers downstream of this FIB

permissions: insert corresponding finance_outbox row in the same transaction
restrictions: must not introduce unregistered event_type values

permissions: deliver FinancialOutboxEventDTO events to registered internal consumers
mark delivered outbox rows as processed
restrictions: exact runtime topology and tuning deferred to PRD / EXEC-SPEC

permissions: apply projection side effect in the same transaction as idempotency record
restrictions: single internal consumer path assumed for Wave 2
must not upgrade origin_label from estimated to actual
must not infer contract fields from payload content
must not add operator-visible outcomes without FIB amendment
must not add external consumer contracts without FIB amendment

trigger_events.name: class_a_financial_transaction_authored
preconditions: rpc_create_financial_txn is invoked through the owning authoring path
event_type is registered in the Wave 2 event catalog

trigger_events.name: class_a_financial_adjustment_authored
preconditions: rpc_create_financial_adjustment is invoked through the owning authoring path
event_type is registered in the Wave 2 event catalog

trigger_events.name: class_b_grind_observation_authored
preconditions: rpc_record_grind_observation is invoked through the owning authoring path
event_type is registered in the Wave 2 event catalog

trigger_events.name: dependency_event_fill_or_credit_requested
preconditions: rpc_request_table_fill or rpc_request_table_credit is invoked through the owning path
event_type is registered in the Wave 2 event catalog

trigger_events.name: relay_poll_cycle_invoked
preconditions: finance_outbox contains zero or more unprocessed rows

trigger_events.name: projection_rebuild_requested
preconditions: consumer rebuild logic uses the same ordered event history

timing_constraints: Outbox row insertion occurs before the authoring transaction commits.
Authoring row and outbox row become durable and visible together.
Relay delivery is polling-based and at-least-once.
Per-entity event history uses ordered replay semantics.
Exact batch size, timeout, lock primitive, and runtime topology are downstream PRD / EXEC-SPEC scope.

business_rules.statement: Every in-scope authoring write must produce a corresponding finance_outbox row in the same database transaction.
Class A and Class B producer wiring must land simultaneously; asymmetric rollout violates ADR-055 parity.
Relay delivery is internal-only, authenticated, polling-based, at-least-once, and concurrency-safe.
Consumers must use processed_messages idempotency before applying side effects.
origin_label is provenance and must not be upgraded by consumers.
Fills and credits are Dependency Events with fact_class operational, origin_label estimated, and player_id null.
New event_type values must be registered in the Wave 2 event catalog before producer RPC or consumer use.
Runtime topology, batching strategy, lock primitive, route location, timeout tuning, and harness internals are not frozen by this FIB unless required for semantic correctness.

invariants: I1: Atomicity — authoring row and finance_outbox row commit or roll back together.
I2: Durability — committed outbox rows survive relay worker crash and are delivered later.
I3: Idempotency — duplicate event_id delivery produces one consumer side effect.
I4: Replayability — ordered outbox history can rebuild equivalent projection state under the same event history.
All new tables carry casino_id where required by SRM contract policy.
No producer or consumer may introduce arbitrary ad-hoc event_type values.
No implementation may turn the outbox into a financial settlement or reconciliation mechanism.
decision_notes: FIB-H is the human scope authority; FIB-S is the machine-legible structured companion per the Zachman-interpolated recommendation.
The structured schema uses What / How / Where / Who / When / Why as a completeness grid, not a full Zachman process.
Unknown implementation details must remain unknown and cannot be invented by downstream artifacts.
This FIB freezes transport semantics and infrastructure boundaries only.

loop.action: execute Class A, Class B, or Dependency Event authoring write
loop.system_response: insert both authoring row and corresponding finance_outbox row before committing the database transaction

loop.action: commit or roll back authoring transaction
loop.system_response: make both rows durable and visible together, or roll back all partial state
retrieve unprocessed events in ordered polling model using concurrency-safe row claiming

loop.action: deliver event to registered internal consumer
loop.system_response: send full FinancialOutboxEventDTO envelope including fact_class, origin_label, event_id, and payload
discard duplicate event without side effect when already processed

loop.action: record new processed message and apply projection side effect
loop.system_response: commit idempotency record and projection change atomically

loop.action: crash or restart before marking processed
loop.system_response: leave row unprocessed so it is redelivered; consumer idempotency prevents duplicate side effect
rebuild projection state equivalent to live projection state under the same ordered event history

required_existing: ADR-052 — financial fact model dual layer
ADR-053 — financial authority and provenance labeling
ADR-054 — financial event propagation surface contract
missing_dependencies: Downstream Wave 2 event catalog artifact for centrally governed event_type values
Downstream PRD / EXEC-SPEC choice of runtime topology, batching strategy, lock primitive, route location, timeout tuning, and harness internals
assumptions: Wave 2 assumes a single internal consumer path for processed_messages.
Multi-consumer fan-out is out of scope and may require future schema evolution.
Relay worker execution remains internal-only and authenticated.
FIB-H status remains draft until human sign-off is recorded.
No downstream artifact may add entities, capabilities, actors, touchpoints, or operator outcomes absent from FIB-H/FIB-S without amendment.

outcomes.statement: Class A authoring writes and finance_outbox rows commit in the same transaction.
Class B authoring writes and finance_outbox rows commit with Class A parity.
Committed finance_outbox rows survive worker failure and are delivered later.
Duplicate event_id delivery produces exactly one consumer side effect.
Projection consumers can rebuild equivalent projection state from ordered history.
Outbox rows expose required envelope fields without payload inference.
origin_label is immutable from authoring boundary to consumer.
event_type values are centrally governed before use.

amendment_required_if_downstream_propose: new operator-visible outcome not described in FIB-H §F or this FIB-S outcomes list
external consumer contract or public event API
new top-level surface, channel, or automation path
new actor or workflow not represented in the containment loop
projection surface work inside this transport FIB
authority reclassification for fills or credits
ad-hoc event_type values not registered in the Wave 2 event catalog
